showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

CourseTimelineClassification.title: CourseTimelineClassification
CourseTimelineClassification.description: Timeline classification filter for enrolled courses.

CourseTimelineSortField.title: CourseTimelineSortField
CourseTimelineSortField.description: Field used to sort enrolled course results.

UserProfileField.title: UserProfileField
UserProfileField.description: User profile field to match against when searching for users.

MoodleWarning.title: MoodleWarning
MoodleWarning.description: A non-fatal warning returned alongside a successful response.

item.description: Item identifier related to the warning; empty string if not applicable.

itemid.description: Numeric item ID related to the warning; 0 if not applicable.

warningcode.description: Machine-readable warning code; empty string if no code.

SiteNotification.title: SiteNotification
SiteNotification.description: A site-level notification (structure varies by type).

id.description: Unique identifier for the site notification.

MoodleError.title: MoodleError
MoodleError.description: Error returned by the LMS on a failed operation.

message.example: Can't find data record in database table.

link.description: Redirect URL for the user; empty string if not applicable.

moreinfourl.description: URL to additional error documentation; empty string if not available.

debuginfo.description: Additional debug information; empty string when not in debug mode.

fullname.description: Full display name of the course.
fullname.example: CS-212 Object Oriented Programming BSCS-13 2K23 ABC

shortname.description: Short course code or abbreviated name.

idnumber.description: Institution-assigned course ID number; empty string if not set.

summaryformat.description: Format of the summary field (1 = HTML).

startdate.description: Course start date as Unix timestamp.

enddate.description: Course end date as Unix timestamp. 0 means no end date.

visible.description: Whether the course is visible to enrolled students.

fullnamedisplay.description: Display name of the course (may differ from fullname for context).
fullnamedisplay.example: CS-212 Object Oriented Programming BSCS-13 2K23 ABC

viewurl.description: URL to the course page on the LMS portal.

progress.description: Student's completion progress as a percentage.

hasprogress.description: Whether completion tracking is enabled for this course.

isfavourite.description: Whether the user has marked this course as a favourite.

hidden.description: Whether the user has hidden this course from their dashboard.

showshortname.description: Whether the short name is displayed alongside the full name.

coursecategory.description: Name of the category this course belongs to.

RecentCourseFields.title: RecentCourseFields
RecentCourseFields.description: Additional fields specific to recently accessed courses.

timeaccess.description: Unix timestamp of the user's most recent access to this course.

RecentCourse.title: RecentCourse
RecentCourse.description: A recently accessed course, extending Course with the last-access timestamp.

fullname.description: Full display name of the course.
fullname.example: CS-212 Object Oriented Programming BSCS-13 2K23 ABC

shortname.description: Short course code or abbreviated name.

idnumber.description: Institution-assigned course ID number; empty string if not set.

summaryformat.description: Format of the summary field (1 = HTML).

startdate.description: Course start date as Unix timestamp.

enddate.description: Course end date as Unix timestamp. 0 means no end date.

visible.description: Whether the course is visible to enrolled students.

fullnamedisplay.description: Display name of the course (may differ from fullname for context).
fullnamedisplay.example: CS-212 Object Oriented Programming BSCS-13 2K23 ABC

viewurl.description: URL to the course page on the LMS portal.

progress.description: Student's completion progress as a percentage.

hasprogress.description: Whether completion tracking is enabled for this course.

isfavourite.description: Whether the user has marked this course as a favourite.

hidden.description: Whether the user has hidden this course from their dashboard.

showshortname.description: Whether the short name is displayed alongside the full name.

coursecategory.description: Name of the category this course belongs to.

timeaccess.description: Unix timestamp of the user's most recent access to this course.

courses.description: List of enrolled courses for the current page.

nextoffset.description: Offset to pass on the next page request; -1 when no further pages exist.

EventIcon.title: EventIcon
EventIcon.description: Icon metadata for a calendar event.

key.description: Icon key name used to locate the icon asset.

component.description: Moodle component that owns the icon (e.g., 'assign').

alttext.description: Accessible alt text for the icon image.

EventSubscription.title: EventSubscription
EventSubscription.description: Subscription display settings for a calendar event.

displayeventsource.description: Whether to display the event source label on the calendar.

EventAction.title: EventAction
EventAction.description: Primary action button metadata for a calendar event.

name.description: Display label for the primary action button.

url.description: URL the user should navigate to in order to perform the action.

itemcount.description: Number of items associated with this action.

actionable.description: Whether the action can currently be taken by the user.

showitemcount.description: Whether to display the item count alongside the action label.

CalendarEvent.title: CalendarEvent
CalendarEvent.description: A calendar action event tied to a course activity (e.g., an assignment deadline).

name.description: Display name of the calendar event.

description.description: HTML description body of the event.

descriptionformat.description: Text format of the description (1 = HTML).

location.description: Physical or virtual location of the event; empty string if not specified.

categoryid.description: Course category ID; null if not a category event.

groupid.description: Group ID if the event is group-specific; null otherwise.

userid.description: User ID if the event is user-specific; null otherwise.

repeatid.description: ID of the repeat group for recurring events; null if not recurring.

eventcount.description: Total number of events in the repeat group; null if not applicable.

component.description: Moodle component that created the event (e.g., 'mod_assign').

modulename.description: Short name of the Moodle activity module (e.g., 'assign').

timestart.description: Event start time as Unix timestamp.

timeduration.description: Duration in seconds; 0 for point-in-time events.

timesort.description: Sort timestamp used for ordering events.

visible.description: Visibility flag for a calendar event. 1 means the event is visible; 0 means it is hidden.

timemodified.description: Last modification time as Unix timestamp.

canedit.description: Whether the current user can edit this event.

candelete.description: Whether the current user can delete this event.

deleteurl.description: URL to delete this calendar event.

editurl.description: URL to edit the underlying course module associated with this event.

viewurl.description: URL to view this event on the calendar.

isactionevent.description: Whether this is an action event requiring user interaction.

iscourseevent.description: Whether this event is associated with a specific course.

iscategoryevent.description: Whether this event is associated with a course category.

groupname.description: Name of the group for group-specific events; null otherwise.

normalisedeventtype.description: Normalised event type identifier used for display grouping.

normalisedeventtypetext.description: Human-readable label for the normalised event type.

url.description: Direct URL to the course activity associated with this event.

CalendarEventsResponse.title: CalendarEventsResponse
CalendarEventsResponse.description: A list of calendar events with pagination boundary IDs.

events.description: List of calendar action events for the current result set.

firstid.description: Event ID of the first item in the result set.

lastid.description: Event ID of the last item in the result set.

CustomField.title: CustomField
CustomField.description: A custom profile field value attached to a user.

type.description: Data type of the custom field (e.g., 'text', 'select').

value.description: Value stored in the custom field.

name.description: Display label of the custom field.

shortname.description: Machine-readable shortname identifier for the custom field.

value.description: Preference value as a string. Note that the internal _lastloaded preference is returned as an integer by the LMS.

fullname.description: Full display name of the user.

department.description: Department the user belongs to within the institution.

institution.description: Name of the institution the user is affiliated with.

idnumber.description: Institution-assigned student or staff ID number.

auth.description: Authentication method used by the user (e.g., 'ldap', 'manual').

confirmed.description: Whether the user's account has been confirmed.

theme.description: Preferred LMS theme; empty string means use the site default.

timezone.description: Timezone identifier. "99" means use the server default.

profileimageurlsmall.description: URL to the user's small profile image (thumbnail).

profileimageurl.description: URL to the user's full-size profile image.

customfields.description: List of custom profile field values for the user.

preferences.description: List of stored preference key/value pairs for the user.

UserPreferencesResponse.title: UserPreferencesResponse
UserPreferencesResponse.description: Response envelope for the user-preferences endpoint.

preferences.description: List of user preference key/value pairs.

warnings.description: Array of warning objects. Empty when no warnings are present.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: A popup notification message delivered to a user.

shortenedsubject.description: Shortened version of the notification subject for compact display.

text.description: Short HTML excerpt of the notification.

fullmessage.description: Full plain-text body of the notification.

fullmessageformat.description: Format identifier for fullmessagehtml (2 = Markdown source).

fullmessagehtml.description: Full HTML body of the notification email.

smallmessage.description: Short plain-text preview of the notification.
smallmessage.example: Areeba Rameen posted in CS-419-Deep Learning...

contexturl.description: URL to the relevant context for this notification (e.g., a forum post).

contexturlname.description: Human-readable label for the context URL link.
contexturlname.example: Lab Sessional Finalization - (DL - Section D & E)

timecreated.description: Unix timestamp when the notification was created.

timecreatedpretty.description: Human-readable relative time string (e.g., '19 days ago').

timeread.description: Unix timestamp when the notification was read; null if unread.

read.description: Whether the notification has been read by the recipient.

deleted.description: Whether the notification has been deleted by the recipient.

iconurl.description: URL to the icon image representing the notification source.

component.description: Moodle component that generated the notification (e.g., 'mod_forum').

eventtype.description: Event type within the component that triggered the notification (e.g., 'posts').

customdata.description: JSON-encoded string with additional notification metadata (cmid, instance, discussionid, postid, etc.).

PopupNotificationsResponse.title: PopupNotificationsResponse
PopupNotificationsResponse.description: Response envelope for the popup-notifications endpoint.

notifications.description: List of popup notifications for the user.

unreadcount.description: Total number of unread notifications for the user.

GetRecentCoursesRequest.title: GetRecentCoursesRequest
GetRecentCoursesRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving recently accessed courses.

limit.description: Maximum number of courses to return.

GetEnrolledCoursesByTimelineRequest.title: GetEnrolledCoursesByTimelineRequest
GetEnrolledCoursesByTimelineRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving enrolled courses filtered by timeline classification.

limit.description: Maximum number of courses to return.

FetchNotificationsRequest.title: FetchNotificationsRequest
FetchNotificationsRequest.description: Request parameters for fetching site-level notifications.

contextid.description: Moodle context ID for which to fetch notifications. The user context ID can be found in the user profile image URL path segment.

GetCalendarEventsByTimesortRequest.title: GetCalendarEventsByTimesortRequest
GetCalendarEventsByTimesortRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving calendar action events sorted by time.

limitnum.description: Maximum number of events to return.

timesortfrom.description: Only return events with timesort >= this Unix timestamp. Pass 0 for no lower bound.

timesortto.description: Only return events with timesort <= this Unix timestamp. Optional.

aftereventid.description: Return events whose ID is greater than this value (cursor pagination). Optional.

GetCalendarEventsByCourseRequest.title: GetCalendarEventsByCourseRequest
GetCalendarEventsByCourseRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving calendar action events for a specific course.

courseid.description: ID of the course to fetch events for.

timesortfrom.description: Only return events with timesort >= this Unix timestamp. Optional.

timesortto.description: Only return events with timesort <= this Unix timestamp. Optional.

aftereventid.description: Cursor-based pagination — return events after this event ID. Optional.

limitnum.description: Maximum number of events to return. Optional.

GetUsersByFieldRequest.title: GetUsersByFieldRequest
GetUsersByFieldRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving user profiles by a profile field value.

values.description: List of field values to look up. All values must be provided as strings even when the field is numeric (e.g., "162154" for an integer ID).

GetUserPreferencesRequest.title: GetUserPreferencesRequest
GetUserPreferencesRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving user preferences.

userid.description: ID of the user whose preferences to retrieve.

name.description: Specific preference name to retrieve. Omit to retrieve all preferences.

GetPopupNotificationsRequest.title: GetPopupNotificationsRequest
GetPopupNotificationsRequest.description: Request parameters for retrieving popup notifications for a user.

useridto.description: ID of the recipient user, passed as a string.

limit.description: Maximum number of notifications to return.

sesskey.description: Session key obtained after authenticating with the LMS portal.

Title: Getting Started with NUST LMS API

Nodes.Text: NUST Learning Management System API for developer integrations (Moodle AJAX wrapper). Each path is virtual for clean SDK generation; NustDevKit resolves them to the underlying Moodle AJAX service endpoint transparently.
The generated code depends on a few Ruby gems. The references to these gems are added in the gemspec file. The easiest way to resolve the dependencies, build the gem and install it is through Rake:

1. Install Rake if not already installed: `gem install rake`
  2. Install Bundler if not already installed: `gem install bundler`
  3. From terminal/cmd navigate to the root directory of the SDK.
  4. Invoke: `rake install`
Alternatively, you can build and install the gem manually:

1. From terminal/cmd navigate to the root directory of the SDK.
  2. Run the build command: `gem build nust_lms_api.gemspec`
  3. Run the install command: `gem install nust_lms_api-1.0.0.gem`

The following section explains how to use the nust_lms_api ruby gem in a new Rails project using RubyMine™. The basic workflow presented here is also applicable if you prefer using a different editor or IDE.
Close any existing projects in RubyMine™ by selecting `File -> Close Project`. Next, click on `Create New Project` to create a new project from scratch.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new project in RubyMine - Step 1

Nodes.Text: Next, provide `TestApp` as the project name, choose `Rails Application` as the project type, and click `OK`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Rails Application in RubyMine - Step 2

Nodes.Text: In the next dialog make sure that the correct Ruby SDK is being used (>= 2.6 and <= 3.2) and click `OK`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Rails Application in RubyMine - Step 3

Nodes.Text: In order to use the `nust_lms_api` gem in the new project we must add a gem reference. Locate the `Gemfile` in the Project Explorer window under the `TestApp` project node. The file contains references to all gems being used in the project. Here, add the reference to the library gem by adding the following line: `gem 'nust_lms_api', '1.0.0'`

Nodes.AlternateText: Add new reference to the Gemfile

Nodes.Text: Once the `TestApp` project is created, a folder named `controllers` will be visible in the *Project Explorer* under the following path: `TestApp > app > controllers`. Right click on this folder and select `New -> Run Rails Generator...`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Run Rails Generator on Controllers Folder

Nodes.Text: Selecting the said option will popup a small window where the generator names are displayed. Here, select the `controller` template.
Next, a popup window will ask you for a Controller name and included Actions. For controller name provide `Hello` and include an action named `Index` and click `OK`.
A new controller class named `HelloController` will be created in a file named `hello_controller.rb` containing a method named `Index`.

1. Add the `require 'nust_lms_api'` statement to require the gem in the controller file.
  2. Add the `include NustLmsApi` statement to include the sdk module in the controller file.
  3. In the `Index` method, add code for initialization and a sample for its usage.
The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:

Data: The Faraday connection object passed by the SDK user for making requests
The Faraday adapter object passed by the SDK user for performing http requests

The value to use for connection timeout.
**Default: 30**

The number of times to retry an endpoint call if it fails.
**Default: 0**

The amount to multiply each successive retry's interval amount by in order to provide backoff.
**Default: 2**

A list of HTTP methods to retry.
**Default: %i[get put get put]**
The Http CallBack allows defining callables for pre and post API calls.
Optional proxy configuration to route HTTP requests through a proxy server.
The SDK logging configuration for API calls
The credential object for Custom Query Parameter

Nodes.Text: The API client can be initialized as follows:
Code-Based Client Initialization
Environment-Based Client Initialization

require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi

# Create client from environment
client = Client.from_env
See the [```Environment-Based Client Initialization```]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Environment-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`SessKey (Custom Query Parameter)`]($h/__auth_CustomQueryAuthentication)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for SessKey.

Data: Session key obtained after authenticating with the LMS portal.

Nodes.Text: **Note:** Auth credentials can be set using named parameter for any of the above credentials (e.g. `sesskey`) in the client initialization.
You must provide credentials in the client as shown in the following code snippet.

require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi

client = Client.new(
  custom_query_authentication_credentials: CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials.new(
    sesskey: 'sesskey'
  )
)

* [Calendar]($e/Calendar)

An instance of the `CoursesApi` class can be accessed from the API Client.
courses_api = client.courses

Nodes.Title: Get Core Recent Courses
Nodes.Name: get_core_recent_courses
Nodes.Description:
Returns the courses the authenticated user has accessed most recently, ordered by last access time.
Moodle method: `core_course_get_recent_courses`
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```Array[RecentCourse]```]($m/RecentCourse).

Parameters.Description: Parameters for the recent courses request.

Fields.Name: limit
Fields.Description: Maximum number of courses to return.

Nodes.Title: Get Enrolled Courses by Timeline
Nodes.Name: get_enrolled_courses_by_timeline
Nodes.Description:
Returns all courses the user is enrolled in, filtered by timeline classification and sorted by a chosen field. Supports pagination via offset.
Moodle method: `core_course_get_enrolled_courses_by_timeline_classification`

Parameters.Description: Parameters for the enrolled courses timeline request.

Fields.Description: Zero-based pagination offset.

Fields.Description: Timeline classification filter for enrolled courses.

Fields.Name: sort
Fields.Description: Field used to sort enrolled course results.

Nodes.Title: Get Calendar Events by Timesort
Nodes.Name: get_calendar_events_by_timesort
Nodes.Description:
Returns action events across all enrolled courses, ordered by their sort timestamp. Use `timesortfrom` to filter to upcoming events only.
Moodle method: `core_calendar_get_action_events_by_timesort`
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```CalendarEventsResponse```]($m/CalendarEventsResponse).

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.Description: Parameters for the time-sorted calendar events request.

Fields.Name: limitnum
Fields.Description: Maximum number of events to return.

Fields.Name: timesortfrom
Fields.Description: Only return events with timesort >= this Unix timestamp. Pass 0 for no lower bound.

Fields.Name: timesortto
Fields.Description: Only return events with timesort <= this Unix timestamp. Optional.

Fields.Name: aftereventid
Fields.Description: Return events whose ID is greater than this value (cursor pagination). Optional.

Nodes.Text: User profile and preferences operations
An instance of the `UsersApi` class can be accessed from the API Client.
users_api = client.users

Nodes.Title: Get Users by Field
Nodes.Name: get_users_by_field
Nodes.Description:
Retrieves one or more user profiles by matching a specific profile field. Commonly used to look up the authenticated user's own profile by their ID.
Moodle method: `core_user_get_users_by_field`
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```Array[UserProfile]```]($m/UserProfile).

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.Description: Parameters specifying the profile field and values to match.

Fields.Name: field
Fields.Description: User profile field to match against when searching for users.

Fields.Name: values
Fields.Description: List of field values to look up. All values must be provided as strings even when the field is numeric (e.g., "162154" for an integer ID).

Nodes.Text: Notification retrieval operations
An instance of the `NotificationsApi` class can be accessed from the API Client.
notifications_api = client.notifications

Nodes.Title: Fetch Notifications
Nodes.Name: fetch_notifications
Nodes.Description:
Returns pending site-level notifications for the given Moodle context. Returns an empty array when there are no pending notifications.
Moodle method: `core_fetch_notifications`
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```Array[SiteNotification]```]($m/SiteNotification).

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.Description: Parameters specifying the context to fetch notifications for.

Fields.Name: contextid
Fields.Description: Moodle context ID for which to fetch notifications. The user context ID can be found in the user profile image URL path segment.

UsageExample.HttpCallTemplate: {"method":"POST","url":"https://lms.nust.edu.pk/portal/service/core_fetch_notifications{% capture inline_query_capture_876234 %}{% if auth["SessKey"]["sesskey"] != nil %}&sesskey={% if auth["SessKey"]["sesskey"] != nil %}{{ auth["SessKey"]["sesskey"] | url_encode }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endcapture %}{% if inline_query_capture_876234.size > 0 %}?{% endif %}{{ inline_query_capture_876234 | remove_first:'&' }}","headers":{% capture header_capture_9023987234 %},"Accept":"application/json","Content-Type":"application/json"{% endcapture %}{{ '{' }}{{ header_capture_9023987234 | remove_first:',' }}},"body":{{ '{' }}"type":"json","data":{% if call["args"]["body"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["body"] | unwrap_add_props | json: false }}{% else %}null{% endif %}}}

Response.Name: response200
Response.Description:
Array of site notifications (empty array when none are pending).

This endpoint returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```Array[SiteNotification]```]($m/SiteNotification).

Nodes.Title: Get Popup Notifications
Nodes.Name: get_popup_notifications
Nodes.Description:
Returns the most recent popup notifications sent to a user, with support for pagination. Also returns the total count of unread notifications.
Moodle method: `message_popup_get_popup_notifications`
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```PopupNotificationsResponse```]($m/PopupNotificationsResponse).

Parameters.Name: body
Parameters.Description: Parameters for the popup notifications request.

Fields.Name: useridto
Fields.Description: ID of the recipient user, passed as a string.

Fields.Name: limit
Fields.Description: Maximum number of notifications to return.

Fields.Name: offset
Fields.Description: Pagination offset.

Nodes.Text: Represents the proxy server configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: The port to connect to the proxy server.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example
CodeExamples.Text:
require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi


client = NustLmsApi::Client.new(
  proxy_settings: ProxySettings.new(
    address: "http://localhost",
    port: 8888,
    username: 'user',
    password: 'pass'
  )
)

Nodes.Text: The SDK client can also be initialized directly from environment variables using the `from_env` class method. This allows the SDK to automatically read configuration values from the runtime environment or a `.env` file.

require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi

# Create client from environment
client = Client.from_env

You can also load values from a `.env` file before creating the client.
To do this, install and use the dotenv
 gem:
gem install dotenv

Now require 'dotenv/load' automatically loads all variables from a `.env` file into `ENV`,
so the `from_env` method can access them.

require 'dotenv/load'
require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi

# Create client from environment
client = Client.from_env
The same method can accept keyword arguments to override any values read from the environment.

# To override or fill in values that are not set in the environment, pass them
# as keyword arguments when calling `from_env`
client = Client.from_env(environment: 'production', timeout: 30)
Values provided through arguments take precedence over those defined in environment variables.
Example .env File

ENVIRONMENT='production'

SESSKEY='sesskey'

LOG_LEVEL=Debug
MASK_SENSITIVE_HEADERS=true
REQUEST_LOG_BODY=true
REQUEST_LOG_HEADERS=true
REQUEST_INCLUDE_QUERY_IN_PATH=true
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Request-ID
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Authorization
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_UNMASK=X-Request-ID
RESPONSE_LOG_BODY=true
RESPONSE_LOG_HEADERS=true
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Correlation-ID,Date,Server
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Set-Cookie,Authorization,X-API-Key
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_UNMASK=X-Correlation-ID

TIMEOUT=60
MAX_RETRIES=3
BACKOFF_FACTOR=2
RETRY_INTERVAL=1
RETRY_STATUSES=408,413
RETRY_METHODS=GET,PUT,DELETE
PROXY_ADDRESS='http://localhost:3000'
PROXY_PORT=8080
PROXY_USERNAME='username'
PROXY_PASSWORD='password'
Note
- If an environment variable is not defined, the default SDK configuration value will be used.
An abstract class for custom logger implementation.
Logger Methods

Data: Logs a message with a specified log level and additional parameters.

1. level (Integer): The log level of the message.
2. message (String): The message template to log.
3. params: (Hash): The parameters to include in the log message.

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Client Initialization with Custom Logger
In order to provide custom logger implementation, the `AbstractLogger` class must be inherited so that you can override the `log` behavior.

require 'ougai'
require 'logger'

include NustLmsApi


class CustomLogger < AbstractLogger
  def initialize
    @logger = Ougai::Logger.new($stdout)
  end
  def log(level, message, params)
    case level
    when Logger::INFO
      @logger.info(message, params)
    end
  end
end
Following is how the custom logger implementation can be injected in the SDK client.

require 'nust_lms_api'
include NustLmsApi

client = Client.new(
  logging_configuration: LoggingConfiguration.new(
    logger: CustomLogger.new
  )
)
Represents the logging configuration for API calls.
Parameters

Data: Takes in your custom implementation of the abstract logger class here. **Default Implementation : `ConsoleLogger`**
Defines the log message severity available in ruby logging module (e.g., DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL, UNKNOWN5). **Default : `Logger::INFO`**
Toggles the global setting to mask sensitive HTTP headers in both requests and responses before logging, safeguarding confidential data. **Default : `true`**
The logging configuration for an API request.
The logging configuration for an API response.
